title: Global macro context feed — US VIX, S&P 500, DAX, crude oil, gold
acceptance_criteria: GlobalMacroFeed class polls Yahoo Finance quote API (https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/{symbol}?interval=5m&range=1d) for five instruments: ^VIX (US CBOE VIX), ^GSPC (S&P 500), ^GDAXI (DAX Germany), CL=F (WTI crude oil futures), GC=F (gold futures)

Poll interval: every MACRO_POLL_INTERVAL_MS (env, default 300000 = 5 minutes). Polls only during configured market hours to avoid wasteful off-hours requests (configurable MACRO_POLL_START / MACRO_POLL_END, default '08:00'/'23:00' IST, since these are global assets)

Each poll stores a MacroDataPoint for each instrument in Redis: redis.set('macro:{key}', JSON.stringify({value, change_pct, timestamp}), 'EX', 900) — TTL 15 minutes (3 missed polls = stale). Keys: 'macro:us_vix', 'macro:sp500', 'macro:dax', 'macro:crude_oil', 'macro:gold'

MacroDataPoint interface exported: { value: number; change_pct: number; timestamp: number } — change_pct is percentage change from the previous session close (computed from Yahoo Finance regularMarketPreviousClose vs regularMarketPrice)

MacroContext interface exported: { us_vix: MacroDataPoint|null; sp500: MacroDataPoint|null; dax: MacroDataPoint|null; crude_oil: MacroDataPoint|null; gold: MacroDataPoint|null } — null when data is absent or stale (> 15 minutes old)

getMacroContext(redis): Promise<MacroContext> exported — reads all five keys from Redis and returns MacroContext. If a key is missing or expired, that field is null. Never throws; log fetch errors at WARN and return null for that instrument
Yahoo Finance HTTP call has a 5-second timeout and retries once on network error. Rate-limit safe (5-minute interval easily within Yahoo Finance's undocumented limits)
GlobalMacroFeed.start(redis) begins the poll loop; GlobalMacroFeed.stop() cancels. Uses the same pattern as VixFeed (clock.tick based timing or real setInterval)

Unit tests: getMacroContext returns null fields when Redis keys are absent; MacroDataPoint parsing correctly extracts value and change_pct from mocked Yahoo Finance response; stale data (expired TTL) returns null field
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
